methods.description: Process for collecting pecan catkins from specific trees, inducing pollen release (dehiscence), filtering debris, weighing the collected pollen, and storing it for subsequent analyses.

steps.description: Samples were taken from each of four trees of 'Stuart' and 'Desirable' pecan growing at the Univ. of Georgia Horticulture Farm, Watkinsville. Catkins were collected on a day between 12:00 PM and 3:00 PM when the anthers had initiated pollen release.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect pecan catkins from specified trees at a specific time window when anthers are releasing pollen.

steps.description: Catkins collected from each tree were weighed
steps.searchable_summary: Weigh the collected catkins from each individual tree.
outputs: Weight data for catkins per tree

steps.description: then spread in single layers on wrapping paper in a laboratory maintained at 27C and 50% RH.
steps.searchable_summary: Spread catkins on paper in a controlled laboratory environment (temperature and humidity).

steps.description: Following a 24h dehiscence period, debris was removed
steps.searchable_summary: Allow a 24-hour period for pollen dehiscence (release).

steps.description: by filtering the pollen through a brass screen with mesh openings of 120 µm.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter the released pollen through a screen with a specific mesh size to remove debris.

steps.description: After determining the weight of pollen collected from each tree,
steps.searchable_summary: Determine the weight of the filtered pollen collected from each tree.
outputs: Weight data for filtered pollen per tree

steps.description: pollen was stored at -80C until used for analyses of the kinetics of pecan pollen dehydration and drying.

methods.description: Process for creating a single composite pollen sample by pooling catkins from multiple 'Stuart' trees, allowing dehiscence, and collecting the released pollen. This composite sample is used for specific experiments like determining the effects of drying on viability.

steps.description: catkins from the 'Stuart' trees were spread on wrapping paper without regard to source tree.
steps.searchable_summary: Pool catkins from 'Stuart' trees and spread on wrapping paper.

steps.description: The pollen released after another 24 h was then collected
steps.searchable_summary: Allow another 24-hour period for pollen release from the pooled catkins.
outputs: Pooled catkins after dehiscence (with released pollen)

steps.description: to form a composite pollen from all four trees.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect the released pollen to form a single composite sample from the pooled sources.

methods.method_name: Pecan Pollen Sample Preparation for Drying
methods.description: Process for preparing sub-samples of the composite 'Stuart' pollen for drying experiments aimed at determining initial moisture content and analyzing drying time effects on viability.

steps.description: The composite 'Stuart' pollen was divided into samples of ≈0.7 g each.
steps.searchable_summary: Divide the composite pollen sample into smaller sub-samples of approximately 0.7 grams each.
